pattern: A block of length m that repeats immediately k times in a row can be found by comparing each position to the one m slots ahead and counting how long that agreement streak runs.
hints: Think about what it means for position i to be the start of a matching repeat: the value at i must equal the value m positions later -- checking that single comparison across the whole array tells you everywhere a repeat boundary lines up.
Slide across the array comparing arr[i] to arr[i+m]; keep a running streak of how many consecutive positions agree, and reset that streak to zero the moment they disagree.

A streak of length m*(k-1) consecutive agreements is exactly enough to prove k full non-overlapping copies of some length-m block sit back to back, so return true as soon as the streak reaches that threshold.

edge_inputs.description: minimal array length, pattern of length 1 repeated exactly twice
m*k equals the array length exactly, a perfect tight fit
m*k is one more than the array length, so it's structurally impossible
a two-element pattern repeated three times back to back with one leftover element
an almost-matching repeat where the last block differs in only one position
every element identical, which trivially satisfies any pattern length and repeat count that fit
a pattern that appears but only reaches k-1 repeats, one short of enough
maximum-length array filled with the maximum value, repeated the maximum number of times

stress.description: fully random arrays and random pattern/repeat sizes, exercising both true and false outcomes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, n // 2))
    max_k = max(2, n // max(1, m))
    k = rng.randint(2, max_k) if max_k >= 2 else 2
    return [arr, m, k]

stress.description: arrays constructed with a guaranteed repeated block, padded with random noise
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, n // 4)) or 1
    k = rng.randint(2, max(2, n // max(1, m)))
    block = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(m)]
    core = block * k
    pad_len = max(0, n - len(core))
    pad = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(pad_len)]
    arr = (core + pad)[:n]
    return [arr, m, k]
stress.note: the constructed core guarantees a true case is reachable for the given m,k
